commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6a6e08cc103e2b11e68b171cd070e9d545325c0b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an opt-in native Talk gateway-relay realtime path, final-speech side-channel delivery with HUD mirroring, response-envelope parsing, provider SDK `speakText` types, tests, and Talk docs.

PR surface: Source +570, Tests +449, Docs +5, Other +1281. Total +2305 across 39 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main review finding: PR-head source unconditionally calls `speakText`, and PR-head search shows no bundled real realtime voice provider implements it. I did not run the signed macOS app flow.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer/author changes for provider speech compatibility, config/docs alignment, and signed macOS proof; automation cannot supply the missing real setup proof or decide the SDK contract alone.

target.title: feat(talk): add macOS realtime relay mode
target.body:
## Summary

- add a provider-neutral macOS Talk realtime relay path backed by `talk.session.*` gateway RPCs
- require explicit `talk.realtime.mode = realtime` plus `talk.realtime.transport = gateway-relay` before native macOS/Android Talk enters the realtime relay path
- keep the existing native STT/TTS fallback when realtime relay config is missing or uses another transport
- deliver OpenClaw brain/finalizer results back to the active realtime Talk session through a Talk-specific final-speech side channel
- require realtime providers to implement `speakText` for final speech; do not emulate final speech by sending provider-visible user messages
- parse structured Talk finals so the voice layer speaks only the `response`/`spokenText` field
- mirror realtime final-speech state to the configured HUD endpoint as best-effort status
- preserve typed relay tool payload/result values at the `AnyCodable` boundary
- keep generic `chat.send` request/schema semantics unchanged; the prior `runtimePromptContext` request-field approach has been removed

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- PR-head diff scan for `runtimePromptContext`, `RuntimePromptContext`, and `chat.send.runtime` in the chat-send/protocol surface: no matches after `6a6e08cc103e`
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/pnpm tsgo:core`
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin:$PATH /private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/pnpm build:strict-smoke`
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/talk-final-hud-mirror.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-chat-final.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`: 12 files, 158 tests passed
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`: 34 tests passed
- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter TalkPromptBuilderTests`: 4 tests passed
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeGatewayConfigTests`: 4 tests passed after resetting stale SwiftPM artifacts and re-fetching cached Sparkle artifacts
- `node /Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw/dist/index.js health --json`: `ok: true`, no plugin errors
- `node /Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway status --json`: gateway `2026.6.2`, loopback RPC `ok: true`, `admin_capable`

Not run:

- Android Gradle unit test: blocked locally because no Android SDK is configured (`ANDROID_HOME` unset and no `apps/android/local.properties`).

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed:** native Talk should only enter realtime relay when explicitly configured for `gateway-relay`; OpenClaw final text should be delivered through the active realtime Talk provider as final speech; final speech delivery should not add a new `chat.send` request field or route provider TTS through a fake user message.

**PR-head proof available on this machine:**

```text
$ git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD
6a6e08cc103e

$ security find-identity -p codesigning -v
0 valid identities found

$ codesign --verify --strict --verbose=2 apps/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/OpenClaw
apps/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/OpenClaw: valid on disk
apps/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/OpenClaw: satisfies its Designated Requirement

$ codesign -dv --verbose=4 apps/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/OpenClaw
Signature=adhoc
CDHash=d90284ee02e3fa2bd9e9af1d7c92957461d091ef
```

The PR-head macOS package compiles and the SwiftPM-built executable verifies as ad-hoc signed. This machine has no valid Developer ID signing identity, so I cannot honestly claim a Developer ID signed PR-head app bundle or notarized runtime proof from this workstation.

**Live runtime proof available on this machine:**

```text
$ node /Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw/dist/index.js health --json
ok: true
plugins.errors: []
defaultAgentId: brain

$ node /Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway status --json
gateway.version: 2026.6.2
rpc.ok: true
rpc.capability: admin_capable
```

**Live signed app identity currently running:**

```text
$ ps -p 80878 -o pid=,comm=,args=
80878 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw

$ codesign -dv --verbose=4 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
Identifier=ai.openclaw.mac
Authority=Developer ID Application: Peter Steinberger (Y5PE65HELJ)
Authority=Developer ID Certification Authority
Authority=Apple Root CA
TeamIdentifier=Y5PE65HELJ
CDHash=75e27c566a4967ab2811ca690f9b61bb6f153509
Notarization Ticket=stapled
Timestamp=Jun 3, 2026 at 5:21:44 PM

$ codesign -dr - /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
designated => identifier "ai.openclaw.mac" and anchor apple generic and certificate 1[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.2.6] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.13] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[subject.OU] = Y5PE65HELJ

$ spctl --assess --type execute -vv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app: accepted
source=Notarized Developer ID
origin=Developer ID Application: Peter Steinberger (Y5PE65HELJ)
```

**What remains for maintainer/media proof:** a signed PR-head macOS app run showing relay session creation, microphone audio forwarding, assistant output audio playback, cancel/barge-in, omitted-transport fallback, and clean visible transcript text. The code now emits the provider-neutral relay markers needed for that capture, but this workstation cannot provide Developer ID signed app proof.
